full_prompt: Design the Tractive System Accumulator Container (TSAC) enclosure for a student-built electric open-wheel race vehicle. Accumulator total mass (cells + BMS + hardware inside the container) = 80 kg; tractive system DC voltage nominal 400 V (must be <= 600 V DC per governing structural-demonstration rules). Container is a single monolithic TIG-welded aluminum alloy 5083-H116 box (E = 72 GPa, yield = 215 MPa, ultimate = 305 MPa, density = 2660 kg/m^3). Overall outer bounding box 650 mm (X, forward) x 450 mm (Y) x 400 mm (Z); wall thickness to be sized (typical 3 mm for side walls, 4 mm for base). Mounted to chassis at 8 locations using M10 Grade 8.8 bolts on a pattern that distributes load paths. Requirements: (R1) Forward inertial loading: 40 g forward applied to the full 80 kg battery mass = 40 x 80 x 9.81 = 31,400 N distributed through the cell module mounting points into the container structure; max von Mises in container walls and welds <= yield / 1.1 = 195 MPa (weld-zone factor). (R2) Lateral (side) inertial loading: 20 g x 80 kg x 9.81 = 15,700 N; max von Mises <= 195 MPa. (R3) Vertical (up and down) inertial loading: 20 g x 80 kg x 9.81 = 15,700 N; max von Mises <= 195 MPa. (R4) No single container fastener, anchor, or weld may fail under the ultimate load of the worst-case case (1.0 x its case load, since the 40 g / 20 g numbers are already ultimate); fastener shear + tension check via VDI 2230 proxy; utilization < 1.0. (R5) Container mass (excluding cells) <= 15 kg. (R6) The container must electrically isolate the tractive system; no structural bolt penetrates the insulating liner. (R7) First modal frequency hard-mounted at the 8 bolt holes >= 40 Hz (avoid 2x chassis mode ~25 Hz). Primary verification: linear static FEA for R1-R3, bolt check for R4, modal for R7. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required (thermal runaway is a separate safety gate). Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded container (treated as a single FEA body), weld map, and a report listing container mass in kg, max von Mises per load case in MPa, bolt utilization, first modal frequency in Hz.
short_prompt:
Design the Tractive System Accumulator Container (TSAC) for a student-built electric open-wheel race vehicle. The enclosure is a monolithic TIG-welded aluminum 5083-H116 box that holds 80 kg of accumulator contents (cells + BMS + hardware), mounted to the chassis at eight M10 Grade 8.8 bolts in a load-distributing pattern. Tractive-system DC is nominal 400 V (governing rule cap <= 600 V). The container must electrically isolate the tractive system; no structural bolt may penetrate the insulating liner.

Envelope: outer bounding 650 x 450 x 400 mm (X forward x Y x Z), typical 3 mm side walls, 4 mm base.

Load cases (inertial loads applied to the full 80 kg pack through the cell-module mounts).
- LC1 forward 40 g: 31,400 N; weld-zone factor applies.
- LC2 side 20 g: 15,700 N.
- LC3 vertical 20 g (both up and down): 15,700 N.
- LC4 fastener / weld utilization: no single bolt, anchor, or weld may exceed utilization 1.0 under the worst case (VDI 2230-style proxy).
- LC5 modal: hard-mounted at eight bolt holes, first frequency clear of 2 x chassis mode ~25 Hz.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Fastener utilization under combined shear + tension is a VDI 2230 calculation done outside FEA.

prompt.title: Student EV tractive-system battery enclosure
prompt.brief: 5083-H116 welded battery box for 80 kg tractive system; survives 40 g forward / 20 g side / 20 g vertical inertial loads.

boundary_conditions.internal_mass: 80 kg lumped and coupled to cell-mount interior surfaces

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal runaway and venting are covered under a separate safety gate.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Box walls in bending; buckling not driving mode.
